sections.paragraph:
ἥσσων (cp. the deriv. ἡττάομαι; Hom. et al.; ins [SIG 709, 3]; pap [UPZ 113, 12: 156 b.c.; PTebt 105, 36]; LXX [Thackeray 122]), ἥττων (Aristoph., Pla. et al.; ins [SIG 851, 10]; pap [PPetr II, 47, 26: 208 b.c.; PTebt 329, 29] s. Gignac I 147; LXX; TestNapht 2:7 [?]; EpArist 257; Joseph.; Just.; Ath. 24, 5), ον, gen. ονος. Comp. without a positive: pert. to being less on a scale of evaluation, lesser, inferior, weaker ἥς. ἁμαρτία a lesser sin 1 Cl 47:4. ἥς. κίνδυνος 55:6 v.l. ἥς. τόπος Mt 20:28 D=Agr 22.—Subst. ὁ ἥς. Mt 20:28 v.l.; οἱ ἥς. 1 Cl 39:9 (Job 5:4). τὸ ἧσσον (opp. τὸ κρεῖσσον): εἰς τὸ ἧς. συνέρχεσθε (when) you come together (it is) for the worse (but the comp. sense is no longer strongly felt: AFridrichsen, Horae Soederblom. I/1, ’44, 30f) 1 Cor 11:17.—The neut. as adv. less (M. Ant. 3, 2, 6; Jos., Ant. 4, 194; 5, 206; Just., A I, 18, 6 and D. 95, 1) εἰ περισσοτέρως ὑμᾶς ἀγαπῶν ἧσσον ἀγαπῶμαι; if I love you much more, am I on that account to be loved less? 2 Cor 12:15. οὐχ ἧττον (v.l. ἥττονι) 1 Cl 55:6 no less, just so (Just., A I, 18, 6).—DELG s.v. ἦκα (adv.). M-M.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

deriv. deriv. = derived, -ation, -ative (fr.)

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Thackeray Thackeray = HT., A Grammar of the OT in Greek according to the Septuagint—List 6

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

PPetr PPetr = The Flinders Petrie Papyri—List 4

Gignac Gignac = FG., Grammar of Greek Papyri—Lists 4, 6

TestNapht TestNapht = Testament of Naphthali, s. Test12Patr—List 2

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

gen. gen. = genitive

Comp Comp date uncertain—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Agr Agr = Agraphon (non-canonical sayings of Jesus)—List 1

opp. opp. = opposed to, opposite

comp. comp. = comparative(ly)

neut. neut. = neuter (used when “n.” would be ambiguous)

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

  1. Ant. M. Ant. = Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 440
